data.title: A major whale swapped WBTC back to WETH, increasing its ETH holdings by 6.45%.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale (0x455...433E) who converted its ETH holdings of four years to WBTC at the beginning of the year has recently converted its WETH back. On January 3rd, this address converted 14,145.93 WETH to 492.16 WBTC at a ratio of 0.03479, worth $44.195 million; on January 26th, it converted 578.66 WBTC back to 17,706.74 WETH at a ratio of 0.03268, worth $50.33 million. This operation increased its ETH holdings by 6.45%. Currently, this address holds 17,707 WETH and 195.49 WBTC on-chain, with a total value of $67.38 million.

data.title: BNB Chain's Growth Spurs Demand for Fixed Rate DeFi Products
data.description: Crypto KOL Gigiz.eth posted on X about the rapid expansion of meme coins and the impressive growth of BNB Chain following the Fermi upgrade. The upgrade has led to a surge in Total Value Locked (TVL) and user numbers, thanks to low gas fees and high throughput, making it attractive to retail investors and project developers. Despite the excitement, challenges remain in the DeFi sector, particularly with the prevalence of floating interest rates in lending protocols. These rates can fluctuate dramatically, causing uncertainty for both borrowers and lenders, and deterring institutional investors from committing large funds.TermMaxFi recently published an article titled 'Why BNB Chain Needs Fixed Risk and Structured Products,' highlighting key points. Firstly, BNB Chain has quietly become a leading blockchain with active users and a rich ecosystem, yet lacks advanced DeFi tools, especially fixed-rate lending options. Floating rates pose significant risks, with borrowers fearing rate spikes and lenders concerned about zero returns. Fixed rates can stabilize costs and returns, making financial planning more predictable.Structured products, such as one-click looping and leverage strategies, offer users advanced ways to earn interest and amplify returns, while supporting tokenized stock collateral. These institutional-grade strategies could elevate the ecosystem. The maturity of the ecosystem progresses in stages: the first two stages focused on user acquisition and TVL growth, and now the third stage requires fixed risk tools to attract substantial capital and truly take off.For DeFi to transition from a retail playground to mainstream finance, fixed interest rates are essential. Ethereum has seen success with platforms like Pendle and Morpho, and BNB Chain's advantages of low fees and high efficiency make it a natural fit for such developments. Institutional involvement could enhance liquidity, depth, and pricing, strengthening the BNB ecosystem further.The question remains whether fixed interest rates will become the next major narrative by 2026. Community members are encouraged to share their thoughts in the comments section.

data.title: Rieder Leads in Fed Successor Odds, Polymarket Data Shows
data.description:
Polymarket data indicates that BlackRock's Global Fixed Income Chief Investment Officer, Rieder, has a 47% implied probability of succeeding at the Federal Reserve. According to ChainCatcher, this surpasses former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh's 28%, current Fed Governor Christopher Waller's 9%, and White House National Economic Advisor Kevin Hassett's 8%.

The event has seen a total trading volume exceeding $250 million. Rieder's pricing curve accelerated in January, overtaking competitor Kevin Warsh on January 23.

data.title: Global Shift from Speculative Investments to Tangible Assets Observed
data.description: Benjamin Cowen, founder and CEO of Into The Cryptoverse, posted on X about the global trend of moving away from speculative investments towards tangible assets. This shift reflects a broader economic cycle that emphasizes the importance of respecting market dynamics rather than resisting them. Cowen's insights highlight the cyclical nature of financial markets, suggesting that understanding and adapting to these cycles is crucial for investors. As the world transitions to more stable asset classes, the focus remains on navigating these changes strategically.

data.title: Cathie Wood Predicts Bitcoin's Short-Term Consolidation Between $80,000 and $92,000
data.description: Cathie Wood has expressed optimism about Bitcoin's future, suggesting that the cryptocurrency may consolidate between $80,000 and $92,000 in the short term. According to Bitalk News, Wood, who is the CEO of ARK Invest, remains confident in Bitcoin's potential, stating that ARK is fully committed to its positive outlook on the digital currency. She believes that the worst phase of the four-year cycle's downturn is largely over, and Bitcoin is poised to resume its upward trajectory soon.

data.title: One trader, who previously profited over $45,000 using insider information, is now betting $14,000 that INFINEX's market capitalization will be less than $200 million after its launch.
data.description: According to The Poly Nerd, an insider invested $98,285 in the INFINEX sale three weeks ago, ultimately gaining $144,174, a net profit of over $45,000. Regarding the performance on January 30th, this address has accumulated $14,000 in bets against INFINEX, predicting that its fully diluted valuation (FDV) one day after launch will not exceed $200 million or $300 million.

data.title: Mox Bank Receives Approval to Offer Virtual Asset Trading Services
data.description:
Mox Bank, a digital bank in Hong Kong, has announced regulatory approval to upgrade its license to provide virtual asset trading services through integrated accounts. According to ChainCatcher, the bank will initially support Bitcoin and Ethereum trading in U.S. dollars. The trading fee structure is divided into four membership levels, with the Basic level charging a 1.25% commission on virtual asset trades, while the Elite level charges 0.5%.

Mox Bank is the second digital bank to receive approval for virtual asset trading. Previously, ZA Bank launched cryptocurrency trading services in July of last year, allowing transactions in BTC, ETH, SOL, and LINK using Hong Kong dollars or U.S. dollars.

data.title: Bitmain Co-Founder Advocates for Continued Bitcoin Mining Amid AI Shift
data.description: Jihan Wu, co-founder of Bitmain, expressed his preference for maintaining Bitcoin mining operations at the Bloomberg New Economy Forum. According to NS3.AI, Wu emphasized that high-efficiency mining offers stable cash flow and instills confidence among power suppliers. He believes this strategy acts as a hedge and supports investments in AI infrastructure during the current market phase. Wu's stance comes as some miners transition to AI infrastructure, highlighting the ongoing relevance of Bitcoin mining in the evolving technological landscape.

data.title: Insider Whale Account Experiences Significant Profit Decline
data.description: An insider whale account has seen its total profits drop to the lowest level since October 2025, following a week of continuous profit-taking. According to Foresight News, the account currently holds long positions valued at $789 million, with an unrealized loss of $73.59 million. Earlier today, the loss briefly approached $90 million.

data.title: Pump.fun co-founder: PENGUIN's early success proves that on-chain transactions have never died.
data.description: Pump.fun co-founder Alon published an article on the X platform stating that PENGUIN's early success proves that on-chain transactions have never died; they are merely a sleeping giant waiting for the right moment. The psychological barriers are only just beginning to be broken down, and the future is full of opportunities.

data.title: Aperture Finance Contract Suspected of Attack
data.description: Aperture Finance's contract is suspected to have been attacked, according to BlockSec monitoring. According to Foresight News, earlier reports from PeckShield indicated that Matcha Meta reported a security breach in SwapNet, resulting in losses amounting to $16.8 million.

data.title: Kalshi's January Trading Volume Projected to Reach $9.1 Billion
data.description: KalshiData has reported on the X platform that as of January 23, the monthly nominal trading volume for Kalshi is approximately $6.7 billion. According to Odaily, the average daily trading volume stands at around $293 million. If the current pace continues, Kalshi's nominal trading volume for January is expected to reach approximately $9.1 billion.

data.title: Kalshi Experiences Significant Growth in Open Contracts Over Six Months
data.description: Kalshi has seen remarkable growth in the past six months, according to Odaily. The platform's open contracts have quadrupled, reaching $410 million, consistently hitting new highs. Sports-related contracts have increased from 25% to 50% of the total, while political contracts remain steady at 25%, with other categories making up the remaining 25%.

data.title: Kalshi's open interest reached $410 million, a fourfold increase over the past six months, with sports-related contracts accounting for 50%.
data.description: Crypto data analyst Dash posted on the X platform that Kalshi has seen significant growth over the past six months. Its open interest has quadrupled to $410 million and continues to break new records. Of this, sports-related open interest rose from 25% to 50%, political open interest remained at 25%, and other categories accounted for 25%.

data.title: Data: Kalshi's notional trading volume reached $6.7 billion in January and is expected to reach $9.1 billion by the end of the month.
data.description: KalshiData stated on the X platform that as of January 23, Kalshi's monthly notional transaction volume was approximately $6.7 billion, with a daily average transaction volume of approximately $293 million. If the current pace continues, Kalshi's notional transaction volume in January is expected to reach approximately $9.1 billion.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Companies will be able to conduct IPOs entirely on-chain in the future.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in an article on the X platform that there is a current need to make it easier for private companies to access capital. The surge in demand from large private companies reflects the negative consequences of increased regulation. Currently, companies tend to remain private for extended periods, leading to profits flowing to private or credit investors, often resulting in poor post-IPO stock performance and a lack of liquidity in the early stages of their lifecycle to establish reasonable valuations. Ultimately, companies will be able to conduct public offerings entirely on-chain, which will significantly reduce costs, friction, and improve accessibility.

data.title: Spot silver breaks through $109
data.description: Spot silver continued its upward trend, breaking through $109 per ounce during the session, rising more than $5 on the day, with gains approaching 6%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Spot Silver Prices Surge, Exceeding $109 Per Ounce
data.description: Spot silver prices have continued their upward trend, surpassing $109 per ounce. According to Odaily, the price increased by more than $5 within the day, approaching a 6% rise.

data.title: OTC Whale Acquires Additional 20,000 ETH
data.description: A major OTC whale address has reportedly purchased 20,000 ETH six hours ago, according to Foresight News. This acquisition is valued at approximately $56.13 million. Over the past five days, the whale has accumulated a total of 70,013 ETH, worth around $203.6 million.
